CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JUL-31-2019 CONTINUED TO SEP-11-2019 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT ARROYO REALTY AND DEVELOPMENT, LLC'S MOTION TO VACATE THE DEFAULT ENTERED ON MAY 30, 2019 IS DENIED AS MOOT. WHEN PLAINTIFF FILED THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ON JULY 1, 2019 THAT MADE SUBSTANTIVE CHANGES TO THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT, THAT FILING OPENED UP THE DEFAULT. DEFENDANT NOW HAS A RIGHT TO RESPOND TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, NOT REPORTED.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. HANS W. HERB, ESQ. of Law Office of Hans W. Herb (Tel: 707-576-0757) appearing via CourtCall for plaintiff Kent Magnell;
Atty. DENNIS L. ALTHOUSE, ESQ. of Schimley Althouse LLP (Tel: 626-262-4400) appearing via CourtCall for defendant Arroyo Realty and Development, LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ARROYO REALTY AND DEVELOPMENT, LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:
Defendant Arroyo Realty and Development, LLC's demurrer to plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is sustained in part. (See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-12-2019 Continued To Sep-11-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jul-23-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Dennis Althouse, Esq. 626-262-4400 of Schimley Althouse LLP for Defendant through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ARROYO REALTY AND DEVELOPMENT, LLC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT.

Defendant Arroyo Realty and Development, LLC's motion to vacate the default entered on May 30, 2019 is denied as moot. When Plaintiff filed the amended complaint on July 1, 2019 that made substantive changes to the original complaint, that filing opened up the default. Defendant now has a right to respond to the amended complaint. (See Ford v. Superior Court (1973) 34 Cal.App.3d 338, 342 ["A defendant who is in default as to the original complaint is entitled to service of an amended pleading making a change of substance, as opposed to a change of form, and has a right to answer or demur thereto."].)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Monetary Sanctions Of $13,342.00 For Bad Faith Actions And/Or Tactics; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities And Declaration Of Dennis L. Althouse, Esq. In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jul-23-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Dennis Althouse, Esq. 626-262-4400 of Schimley Althouse LLP() for Defendant through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ARROYO REALTY AND DEVELOPMENT, LLC'S Motion For Monetary Sanctions Of $13,342.00 For Bad Faith Actions And/Or Tactics.

Defendant Arroyo Realty and Development, LLC's motion for sanctions is denied. While the Court does not condone attorney Hans Herb's unethical conduct, the Court cannot grant sanctions under Code of Civil Procedure 128.5. The safe harbor provision of Code of Civil Procedure 128.5(f)(1)(B) applies to this case because Plaintiff could have withdrawn the default prior to Defendant filing this motion. (See CPF Vaseo Associates, LLC v. Gray (2018) 29 Cal.App.5th 997, 1006 [safe harbor provision applied to motion to vacate improperly entered judgment, which could have been subject to corrective action]; Nutrition Distribution, LLC v. Southern SARMS, Inc. (2018) 20 Cal.App.5th 117, 120, 130 [21-day waiting period applies to a motion for sanctions under 128.5 that is directed to allegedly improper actions or tactics that can be withdrawn or appropriately corrected].)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
